name: Boy Scout Rule and Hermetic Test Cleanup
goal: Add a scope-aware Boy Scout Rule to every IronClaude instruction path, make five restricted-runner tests hermetic, and document direct OpenAI Codex support without implying Commander support.

tasks.name: Propagate and guard the Boy Scout Rule
tasks.description: Add the scope-aware directive to current, generated, activation, and Brain instruction surfaces with regression guards.

steps.description: Create commander/tests/test_boy_scout_directive.py with REPO_ROOT/read helper; exact DIRECTIVE_BODY assertions across CLAUDE.md, worker/CLAUDE.md, commander/CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/behavioral.md, and worker template; exact COMPACT_DIRECTIVE and DETECTION_ROW; exact BRAIN_FIX_BUGS directive 16, BRAIN_FIX_BEFORE_REPORTING directive 20, and complete BRAIN_DIRECTIVE 24; activation counts 11 concepts/(11 principles)/11-principle template; and stale 18-count rejection. Use four-space child bullets for two-digit directives.
steps.expected: Fails because the directive is absent.

steps.description: Append the exact five-bullet canonical block from Markdown Task 1 Step 2 as directive 10 in CLAUDE.md, 13 in worker/CLAUDE.md, 11 in commander/CLAUDE.md, 12 in .claude/rules/behavioral.md, and 12 in commander/src/ironclaude/templates/worker_claude_md.md.
steps.expected: Every direct surface has identical evidence, authorized cleanup, destructive/external permission, blocked/unsafe recording, and YAGNI language.

steps.description: In worker/skills/activate-professional-mode/SKILL.md add exact compact directive 11, exact full canonical directive 11, exact semantic row 11, and exact Concept 11 append block from Markdown Task 1 Step 3; replace the single 10 concepts and (10 principles) strings plus every 10-principle template string with 11 equivalents.
steps.expected: All activation propagation vectors are complete and exact.

steps.description: Replace Brain directives 16 and 20 with the exact confirmed-directive and permission boundaries in Markdown Task 1 Step 4; append exact Brain directive 24 including destructive/external and blocked/unsafe branches; replace orchestrator_claude.md's counted behavioral description with the exact count-free description.
steps.expected: Brain directives are internally consistent with operator authorization.

steps.description: Rename worker-template inventory test to test_template_contains_all_twelve_directives with the exact 12-item list; update commander/tests/test_advisor_fallback_directive.py count-pin comments and strings from 10 to 11 without changing Advisor Fallback semantics.
steps.expected: All focused propagation tests pass.

steps.description: Stage every Task 1 allowed file.
steps.command: git add CLAUDE.md worker/CLAUDE.md commander/CLAUDE.md .claude/rules/behavioral.md commander/src/ironclaude/templates/worker_claude_md.md worker/skills/activate-professional-mode/SKILL.md commander/src/brain/rules/behavioral.md commander/src/brain/orchestrator_claude.md commander/tests/test_boy_scout_directive.py commander/tests/test_worker_claude_md_template.py commander/tests/test_advisor_fallback_directive.py
steps.expected: Only Task 1 files are newly staged.

tasks.name: Replace privileged test resources with hermetic seams
tasks.description: Remove live tmux-server and TCP-listener dependencies while preserving behavior coverage.

steps.description: Reproduce the five restricted-runner failures before edits.
steps.expected: Four tmux Operation not permitted failures and one socket.bind PermissionError.

steps.description: Replace commander/tests/test_kill_orphan_workers.py with a MagicMock(spec=TmuxManager), MagicMock(spec=WorkerRegistry), _dependencies(sessions, registered) returning worker dicts, and four exact tests: unregistered calls list_sessions(prefix='ic-') and kill_session; registered and ic-brain never kill; empty list still asserts prefix request and no kill. Remove subprocess/pytest/DB fixtures entirely.
steps.expected: No subprocess, live tmux session, or operator socket access remains.

steps.description: Replace the wiki redirect test with socket.socketpair(); send raw GET /wiki HTTP/1.1 bytes and shutdown client write; construct WikiHandler with SimpleNamespace(server_name='localhost', server_port=80); shutdown handler_socket write after handler returns; recv to EOF; close both sockets in finally; decode iso-8859-1 headers; assert HTTP/1.0 301 prefix and Location: /wiki/ header.
steps.expected: Real HTTP 301 and Location behavior without bind/listen or EOF deadlock.

steps.description: Rerun the exact formerly failing five-test selector in the restricted runner.
steps.expected: 5 passed.

steps.description: Run both complete affected files in the restricted runner.
steps.expected: 7 passed without tmux access, TCP bind, or hang.

steps.description: Stage both Task 2 allowed files.
steps.expected: Only the two hermetic test files are newly staged.

tasks.name: Document compatibility and verify the release
tasks.description: Make the direct OpenAI Codex versus Claude Code-only Commander boundary explicit, document the release changes, and run complete verification.

steps.description: In README replace headline with '**Workflow discipline and multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code and OpenAI Codex.**'; replace opening lead with 'IronClaude adds workflow discipline to Claude Code and OpenAI Codex, plus multi-session Claude Code orchestration.' while retaining its remaining explanation; change Worker lead to 'A Claude Code and OpenAI Codex plugin...'; add exact Compatibility callout; preserve quick-start heading/anchor; replace quick-start first paragraph with exact OpenAI Codex sentence; add exact two v1.0.24 bullets from the human plan.
steps.expected: Compatibility boundary is explicit and existing navigation remains valid.

steps.description: In CHANGELOG replace v1.0.24 summary with the exact sentence in the human plan; add exact Added bullet 'Scope-aware Boy Scout Rule', exact Changed bullet 'Direct OpenAI Codex compatibility is explicit', and exact Fixed bullet 'Restricted-runner tests are hermetic', preserving all existing release entries.
steps.expected: Release notes accurately describe the integrated release.

steps.description: Run focused behavioral, version, and affected-file tests.
steps.expected: All focused tests pass.

steps.description: Validate the Codex plugin manifest.
steps.expected: Plugin validation succeeds.

steps.description: Run all 11 exact hook scripts in one fail-fast loop.
steps.command: for script in worker/hooks/test-bg-detection.sh worker/hooks/test-config-guard-integration.sh worker/hooks/test-config-guard.sh worker/hooks/test-guard-security.sh worker/hooks/test-poll-dedup.sh worker/hooks/tests/test-antipattern-lexicon.sh worker/hooks/tests/test-bash-readonly-guard.sh worker/hooks/tests/test-gbtw-antipattern-override.sh worker/hooks/tests/test-gbtw-inflight.sh worker/hooks/tests/test-gbtw-waiting.sh worker/hooks/tests/test-sad-antipattern.sh; do bash "$script" || exit 1; done
steps.expected: All 11 scripts exit 0.

steps.description: Run the complete Commander suite in the restricted runner.
steps.expected: 1969 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed.

steps.description: Stage release documentation.
steps.expected: README.md and CHANGELOG.md are staged.

steps.description: Inspect worktree scope.
steps.expected: Only approved files are staged and AGENTS.md remains untracked.

steps.description: Inspect staged file statistics.
steps.expected: Only approved design, plan, instruction, test, and release files appear.

steps.description: Check staged whitespace.
steps.expected: No output and exit 0.

steps.description: Verify pre-amend history baseline.
steps.expected: One local v1.0.24 commit sits above origin/main v1.0.23.
